root_motivation: DAX EVALUATE is the primary compile target for the MQO because it returns a flat table (1:1 with JSON array-of-objects, LLM-native) while preserving multidimensional semantics — eliminating the MDX cellset cross-product that burns inference credits. The compiler must be drop-in usable from the mqo pipeline: BoundMqo JSON in, valid DAX EVALUATE string out.
user_persona: AtScale MQO pipeline subagent (or CLI integration) that feeds BoundMqo JSON produced by mqo-bind into mqo-dax and receives syntactically valid DAX EVALUATE text for execution against a Power BI / AtScale DAX engine.

acceptance_criteria.description: At least 8 golden BoundMqo → DAX pairs compile string-equal after normalization.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance.rs — ac1_golden_* (8 cases: measure_only_row, measure_plus_dim, two_measures, member_filter, range_filter, limit_topn, order_by, calc_group_member_filter)

acceptance_criteria.description: Each TimeIntel variant maps to the correct DAX function: YoY→SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR, PriorPeriod→DATEADD, ToDate{Year}→DATESYTD, ToDate{Quarter}→DATESQTD, ToDate{Month}→DATESMTD, RunningTotal→DATESINTORANGE, Share→DIVIDE+ALL, Rank{top_n}→TOPN.
A calc-group member is emitted as a calc-group column filter (CalcGroup[CalcGroup] = 'member'), not invented time-intelligence logic.
limit produces TOPN wrapper; order produces ORDER BY clause with correct ASC/DESC direction.
All emitted DAX passes the bundled structural syntax check (EVALUATE keyword, balanced parens/brackets/quotes, at least one recognized table construct). No engine round-trip required.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance.rs — ac5_* + validate_dax_syntax called on every golden output in all AC1-AC4 tests

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes.
Binary integration test: mqo-dax --bound <fixture.json> emits valid DAX to stdout with exit code 0; missing file exits 2; empty-measures JSON exits 1.
Mutation kill rate >= 60% (Phase 1 telemetry only, not a hard gate).

scope: BoundMqo JSON deserialization via local types (not binder crate dep)
TimeIntel variant mapping to DAX functions
Bundled structural DAX syntax validation (no engine round-trip)
non_goals: MDX compilation — this is the DAX-primary path; MDX is a separate binary
Engine round-trip validation — syntax check is structural only
DAX semantic correctness verification (model schema awareness)

additional.git_dep_mqo_spec: mqo-spec is a path dep from the mqo monorepo; must resolve to the autobuilder-rebuilt version

five_whys_trace.q: Why compile BoundMqo to DAX rather than MDX?
five_whys_trace.a: DAX EVALUATE returns a flat table — JSON array-of-objects — which is directly LLM-native. MDX returns a multidimensional cellset that produces cross-product blow-up (the rowLimitAdvisory in mcp-server proves it burns credits).

five_whys_trace.q: Why is LLM-native output the deciding factor?
five_whys_trace.a: The consumer is an LLM agent (MQO pipeline subagent). It must parse the result in a single token-efficient pass. A flat table is O(rows) tokens; a cellset is O(rows × cols) and requires a reshape step that adds latency and failure modes.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not flatten MDX on the server side and skip a new compiler?
five_whys_trace.a: AtScale's DaxEvaluateHandler already exists and returns tabular results natively. Using the DAX path avoids an extra reshape layer and leverages the SSDAX + Power BI investment already in Joe's charter.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is this a standalone CLI rather than a function inside mqo-server?
five_whys_trace.a: The MQO pipeline is compositional: bind → compile → execute. A CLI binary is independently testable, can be swapped for a future SPARQL or SQL backend, and matches the pipeline's Unix-pipe architecture.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the compiler validate DAX semantics (measure exists in model) or only structure?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Structural only — no engine round-trip required per AC5. The binder (mqo-bind) owns semantic validation.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the git dep path for mqo-spec now that autobuilder rebuilt it?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Cargo.toml uses path = '../mqo-spec' (sibling directory); the autobuilder-rebuilt mqo-spec is in ~/Documents/projects/mqo-spec.
